title: Innovative Approaches to Early Cancer Detection (R01)
description: Supports research developing novel biomarkers and imaging methods for the early detection of solid tumors, with emphasis on translational pipelines from discovery to clinical validation.
eligibility_text: Open to PIs at accredited research institutions. Foreign components allowed with justification.

title: Neural Circuits of Chronic Pain (R21)
description: Exploratory funding for studies mapping the neural circuitry underlying chronic pain states using electrophysiology, optogenetics, or in vivo imaging in model systems.
eligibility_text: Domestic and foreign institutions eligible. Early-stage investigators encouraged.

title: Host Immune Response to Emerging Viral Pathogens (U19)
description: Cooperative agreement funding multi-project programs characterizing innate and adaptive immune responses to emerging RNA viruses, including correlates of protection and vaccine target discovery.
eligibility_text: Requires a multi-PI program structure. U.S. institutions as lead; international partners permitted.

title: Horizon Europe — AI for Precision Cardiology
description: Funds consortia developing trustworthy AI tools for risk stratification and treatment selection in cardiovascular disease, with mandatory clinical validation and explainability requirements.
eligibility_text: Requires a consortium of at least three independent legal entities from three different EU Member States or Associated Countries.

title: Horizon Europe — Genomic Surveillance of Antimicrobial Resistance
description: Supports cross-border platforms for genomic surveillance of antimicrobial-resistant pathogens, integrating sequencing, epidemiology, and open data sharing across health systems.
eligibility_text: Consortium-based. Public health agencies and academic partners encouraged to co-apply.

title: Horizon Europe — Digital Tools for Population Mental Health
description: Funds development and evaluation of scalable digital interventions for population-level mental health, prioritizing equity, accessibility, and real-world effectiveness trials.
eligibility_text: Open to EU and Associated Country entities forming an eligible consortium.

title: Israel Science Foundation — Individual Research Grant in Computational Biology
description: Supports individual investigators pursuing fundamental questions in computational and systems biology, including single-cell analysis, network modeling, and method development.
eligibility_text: Open to faculty at Israeli research institutions. One submission per PI per cycle.

title: Israel Science Foundation — Equipment Grant for Advanced Microscopy
description: Provides shared-infrastructure funding for advanced light and electron microscopy platforms supporting cell-biology and neuroscience research communities.
eligibility_text: Institutional applications for shared equipment. Co-investment from the host institution required.

title: US–Israel Binational Science Foundation — Collaborative Oncology Research
description: Funds joint US–Israel research teams studying tumor microenvironment and immunotherapy resistance, requiring substantive scientific collaboration between the two partners.
eligibility_text: Requires one eligible US investigator and one eligible Israeli investigator as joint PIs.

title: US–Israel Binational Science Foundation — Cardiometabolic Disease Mechanisms
description: Supports binational teams investigating molecular mechanisms linking metabolism and cardiovascular disease, with an emphasis on translational relevance and shared data resources.
eligibility_text: Joint US–Israel PI structure mandatory. Budgets administered separately in each country.
